question: What do Tables 5.3-3 and 5.3-8 reveal about the RPV adjusted reference temperatures and PTS screening?

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the methodology used to calculate these limits, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and presents the resulting pressure-temperature curves and data, which ensure protection against non-ductile failure of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies compliance with ASME BPVC and NQA-1 standards for packaging and handling, maintenance of a dry and inert environment during shipment, and points to other sections for detailed procedures and technical specifications.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures for ensuring the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation over a 60-year design life, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits. The section also notes that procedures will be based on the actual material properties of the as-built reactor vessels.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the core, steam generators, and pressurizer, and serves as a key barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and provides structural support for various reactor components, with materials and fabrication methods selected to ensure integrity and compliance with ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards throughout the plant's design lifetime.

edges.description: Section A discusses compliance with ASME BPVC requirements for RPV attachments and welds, while Section B outlines inspection and handling requirements referencing ASME BPVC and NQA-1 standards, with both sections connected through their reliance on ASME BPVC codes for regulatory compliance.

Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with pressure-temperature limits, which are established based on the analyses and data (such as those in Section A) regarding reactor vessel integrity and ASME code requirements.

Section A discusses specific examination and code compliance requirements for RPV attachments, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV and its role in the reactor coolant system, with both sections connected by their focus on the RPV's structural integrity and regulatory compliance.

Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which is directly related to the adjusted reference temperatures and PTS screening values presented in Tables 5.3-3 and 5.3-8, while Section A references the inspection and handling requirements that ensure the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) maintains its integrity as assumed in those tables.

Section A references the inspection and handling requirements for the RPV, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's structure and function, connecting them through the subject of RPV design and regulatory compliance.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure that reactor pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which is directly dependent on the design and material properties of the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV) described in Section B.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies detailed requirements and analyses related to the RPV's pressure-temperature limits and pressurized thermal shock (PTS) screening, connecting them through the vessel's structural integrity and regulatory compliance.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies the inspection, shipment, and installation requirements that apply to the RPV as described in Section A.

Section A provides the physical and functional context of the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies the requirement to develop operating procedures that ensure the RPV's pressure-temperature limits—such as those derived from adjusted reference temperatures and PTS screening in Tables 5.3-3 and 5.3-8—are not exceeded.
